ocr.text: In recent years, the investment landscape has increasingly prioritized Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) factors,
reflecting a growing recognition of their impact on long-term financial performance. This report presents a comprehensive
analysis of ESG scoring and fund ratings across various funds managed by Bridgewater Associates, aimed at providing
investors with robust insights into the sustainability and ethical performance of their portfolios.

Notably, Fund3, classified as a "Low" fund type, achieved an impressive ESG score of 1.10, indicating its strong commitment
to responsible investment practices. Furthermore, the total assets under management (TotalAUM(USD)) for Fund3 reached
300M, bolstering its position as a viable investment option. Conversely, Fund4 demonstrated a slightly lower performance with
an ESG score of 0.85, highlighting the variability in fund ratings within the portfolio. The analysis also reveals that Fund1,
another significant player, reported a score of 1.0, alongside a substantial AUM of 200M, reflecting its solid standing in the
ESG framework. As we delve deeper into the metrics, it is essential to note that 20M's 11% performance rating was recorded at
1.3, further illustrating the diverse range of fund performances. The insights provided herein will equip stakeholders with the
necessary tools to make informed investment decisions that align with their ESG values.
